name: Tagus River and Barrancas de Burujón Loop
name_original: Monumento Natural Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña – circular desde La Puebla de Montalbán por las Barrancas de Torralba-Burujón
description: This easy 13.5-mile mountain biking route explores the dramatic reddish clay ravines of Barrancas de Burujón near La Puebla de Montalbán.
region_intro: Mountain biking around La Puebla de Montalbán offers a diverse landscape for riders. The region features varied terrain, including paths along the Tagus River and through the distinctive Barrancas de Burujón, characterized by eroded clay formations. Riders can expect a mix of open rural tracks and routes connecting to nearby town centers, providing a blend of natural scenery and cultural points of interest. The area's elevation changes are generally moderate, making it accessible for different skill levels while still offering engaging rides. Best mountain bike trails around La Puebla De Montalbán The most popular mountain bike trail is Tagus River and Barrancas de Burujón Loop, a 13.5 miles (21.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 50 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Tagus River and passes through the unique Barrancas de Burujón. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Torrijos Town Center – La Celestina Museum loop from La Puebla de Montalbán, a moderate 25.2 miles (40.6 km) path. This route connects La Puebla de Montalbán with Torrijos, offering a mix of rural and town-adjacent scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the La Celestina Museum – Torrijos Town Center loop from La Puebla de Montalbán, a 29.1 miles (46.8 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes and connecting cultural sites, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Mountain biking around La Puebla de Montalbán is defined by riverine paths, distinctive barrancas, and open rural tracks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in La Puebla de Montalbán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from around 20 reviews. More than 290 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore La Puebla de Montalbán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in La Puebla de Montalbán?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails available around La Puebla de Montalbán, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in La Puebla de Montalbán?
A: The best times for mountain biking in La Puebla de Montalbán are typically spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable rides along the riverine paths and through the Barrancas de Burujón.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in La Puebla de Montalbán?
A: Yes, there are several easy mountain bike trails in the area. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021988423/tagus-river-and-barrancas-de-burujon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tagus River and Barrancas de Burujón Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 13.5-mile (21.7 km) route that follows the Tagus River and passes through unique clay formations, perfect for those new to mountain biking or looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: While many routes are moderate, there are 8 trails classified as difficult for experienced riders seeking more of a challenge. These routes often feature greater elevation changes and more technical sections, providing a more demanding experience than the easier loops.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around La Puebla de Montalbán?
A: You can expect a diverse landscape, including paths along the Tagus River, routes through the distinctive Barrancas de Burujón with their eroded clay formations, and open rural tracks. The terrain generally features moderate elevation changes, blending natural scenery with connections to nearby town centers.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around La Puebla de Montalbán are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31483812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrijos Town Center – La Celestina Museum loop from La Puebla de Montalbán&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 25.2-mile (40.6 km) loop that offers a mix of rural and town-adjacent scenery.

Q: Where can I park when mountain biking in La Puebla de Montalbán?
A: Parking is available at various points, especially near popular attractions. For example, there is a dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/614475" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parking area at Las Barrancas de Burujón&lt;/a&gt;, which is a convenient starting point for routes exploring that natural monument.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, many routes connect to or pass through town centers like La Puebla de Montalbán and Torrijos, where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33827717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Celestina Museum – Torrijos Town Center loop from La Puebla de Montalbán&lt;/a&gt; offer opportunities to stop in these towns.

Q: What should I wear for mountain biking in La Puebla de Montalbán?
A: Given the region's climate, especially during spring and autumn, lightweight, breathable cycling apparel is recommended. Layers are useful for cooler mornings or evenings. Always bring sun protection, such as sunscreen and sunglasses, and carry enough water, particularly during warmer months.

Q: Are there scenic mountain bike trails with good views?
A: Absolutely. The trails along the Tagus River and through the Barrancas de Burujón are particularly scenic. You can enjoy stunning vistas from viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/614479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enebros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/614477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Barrancas de Burujón and the Castrejón Reservoir from Mirador del Cambrón&lt;/a&gt;, which are accessible from various routes.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in La Puebla de Montalbán?
A: Beyond the natural beauty of the Tagus River and the Barrancas de Burujón, you can encounter various points of interest. These include cultural sites like the La Celestina Museum, and natural landmarks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2675742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Noez Peak&lt;/a&gt; and several viewpoints offering panoramic views of the landscape and the Castrejón Reservoir.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike options?
A: Yes, the easier routes are well-suited for families. The moderate elevation changes and well-defined paths make them enjoyable for riders of different ages and skill levels. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021988423/tagus-river-and-barrancas-de-burujon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tagus River and Barrancas de Burujón Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great example of a family-friendly option.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take in La Puebla de Montalbán?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021988423/tagus-river-and-barrancas-de-burujon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tagus River and Barrancas de Burujón Loop&lt;/a&gt; can take around 1 hour 50 minutes, while longer moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31462752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrijos Town Center – Carmena loop from La Puebla de Montalbán&lt;/a&gt;, might take over 4 hours.

Q: What are the distances of mountain bike routes in La Puebla de Montalbán?
A: The mountain bike routes in La Puebla de Montalbán range from approximately 13.5 miles (21.7 km) for shorter, easier loops to over 32 miles (51 km) for longer, more extensive rides. This variety allows riders to choose a distance that suits their fitness and time availability.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in La Puebla de Montalbán?
A: The trails in La Puebla de Montalbán are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.8 stars from around 20 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic views of the Barrancas de Burujón, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various skill levels.
tips: This wonderful place, declared a Natural Monument, is worth visiting and exploring it completely. With 4 viewpoints connected by a path, you will have beautiful views of the Castrejón reservoir and the Tagus river in each of them, all deserving of a postcard!

This section of path that joins the Cambrón and Enebros viewpoints located at the southern end of the ravine path, offers privileged views of the entire ravine, its curious natural formation and the Castrejón reservoir, a whole dream complex.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain bike route is approximately 21.65 km long and generally takes around 1 hour and 50 minutes to complete. However, this duration can vary depending on your pace, fitness level, and how often you stop to enjoy the spectacular views.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is considered easy or of low difficulty, making it suitable for mountain bikers of all fitness levels and skill ranges. While there might be short, steep climbs in some areas, the overall terrain is manageable.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of dirt tracks ('pista de tierra'), with some calmer sections of asphalt. You'll be riding through areas characterized by spectacular reddish clay ravines ('cárcavas arcillosas') and 'badlands' formations, offering a unique and visually striking experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The route starts from La Puebla de Montalbán. There are car parks available in the vicinity, making it convenient to access the trailhead. Access to some viewpoints within the Natural Monument can also be made by car.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers breathtaking panoramic views of the reddish clay cliffs and the Castrejón reservoir. Key highlights include the Barrancas de Burujón and the Viewpoints of Las Barrancas and Castrejón Reservoir. You'll also find other notable spots like Mirador del Cambrón, Mirador de los Enebros, and the 'Pico del Cambrón', which is the highest point of the ravines.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to visit. In spring, you might see poppies blooming in the cereal fields, adding to the scenic beauty. While accessible year-round, be aware that there is limited shade, so carrying sufficient water is advised, especially during warmer months.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other well-known paths, including the Camino Natural del Tajo (GR 113), the Sendero de la Barranca, and the Cañada Real Segoviana.

faqs.question: Is the Monumento Natural Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña dog-friendly for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: While the Natural Monument is generally accessible, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect the local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors. Please ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to mountain bike in the Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña Natural Monument?
faqs.answer: The Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña is a Natural Monument, but there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for general access to the area or for mountain biking on this route. However, always respect local regulations and protected areas.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and flora can I expect to see along the trail?
faqs.answer: The Barrancas is a protected area for fauna, so you might spot various bird species such as the eagle owl, black vulture, and the rare Iberian imperial eagle. The landscape also features olive groves and cereal fields, which can be particularly vibrant with poppies in the spring.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the limited shade, it's crucial to bring plenty of water, especially during warmer months. Sun protection (sunscreen, hat) is also highly recommended. Basic bike repair tools, a first-aid kit, and snacks are always good to have on any ride.

waypoints.name: Viewpoints of Las Barrancas and Castrejón Reservoir
